# Discovery - ablation Run #1 (no-AI baseline)

**Author:** Johan Pieterse, The Archive and Heritage Group (Pty) Ltd
**Date:** 2026-04-28
**Corpus:** ANC archive (atom DB), 454,393 information_objects; 388,459 carry NER entities (9.79M total).
**Heratio version:** 1.20.0 (sha=1e5d1fd5)
**Queries:** 100 simulated, generated by `DiscoverySimulatedQueryService` (seed=42).
**Qrels SHA-256:** `sha256:1d7ab3bbfd298cc0b48cb8110...`
**Run timestamp (UTC):** 2026-04-28T17:15:35+00:00

Implements the Run #1 deliverable in GitHub issue #11. All four query generators run without LLM paraphrase (Generator C is the rule-based scope-NP slot, not the LLM-paraphrase variant). The full GenDSs paper's Run #2 will swap C for the LLM-paraphrase generator once GPU returns and the ANC PageIndex tree is built.

## Configurations

| Config | Strategies | Defining property |
|---|---|---|
| `baseline` | keyword | ES BM25 over IO title/scope/history (issue #13) |
| `kw_entity` | keyword + entity | + ES BM25 over denormalised NER values (issue #24) |
| `kw_hierarchy` | keyword + hierarchical | + MPTT sibling/child walk on `parent_id` (batched, issue #29) |
| `kw_vector` | keyword + vector | + Qdrant 384-d cosine with all-minilm embeddings, RRF k=60 |
| `full_hybrid` | keyword + entity + hierarchical + vector | all four strategies, weighted merge + RRF for vector |

## Aggregate retrieval quality

Mean ± 95% bootstrap CI (1000 resamples, seeded). Best in column **bold**.

| Config | nDCG@10 | MRR | R@10 | R@50 | R@100 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `baseline` | **0.384<br>(0.294–0.466)** | 0.367<br>(0.286–0.457) | **0.442<br>(0.341–0.530)** | 0.456<br>(0.364–0.557) | 0.456<br>(0.360–0.554) |
| `kw_entity` | 0.183<br>(0.128–0.237) | 0.159<br>(0.111–0.214) | 0.322<br>(0.224–0.402) | 0.398<br>(0.310–0.495) | 0.398<br>(0.308–0.489) |
| `kw_hierarchy` | 0.379<br>(0.289–0.462) | 0.366<br>(0.283–0.457) | 0.431<br>(0.331–0.512) | 0.456<br>(0.364–0.557) | 0.456<br>(0.360–0.554) |
| `kw_vector` | 0.383<br>(0.294–0.465) | **0.368<br>(0.286–0.458)** | 0.441<br>(0.341–0.530) | **0.456<br>(0.364–0.557)** | **0.456<br>(0.360–0.554)** |
| `full_hybrid` | 0.195<br>(0.139–0.250) | 0.166<br>(0.117–0.221) | 0.342<br>(0.251–0.431) | 0.398<br>(0.310–0.495) | 0.398<br>(0.308–0.489) |

| `baseline` | 487 | 509 | 576 | 47 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| `kw_entity` | 545 | 550 | 633 | 46 | 36 | 0 | 0 |

## Per-query-type breakdown - `full_hybrid`

Where each generator's queries are strongest / weakest. Same metric definitions as above.

| query_type | n | nDCG@10 | MRR | Recall@10 | Recall@50 | Recall@100 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| `title` | 30 | 0.415 | 0.302 | 0.800 | 0.900 | 0.900 |
| `subject` | 40 | 0.013 | 0.037 | 0.004 | 0.018 | 0.018 |
| `scope_np` | 20 | 0.255 | 0.217 | 0.400 | 0.500 | 0.500 |
| `typo` | 10 | 0.144 | 0.170 | 0.204 | 0.210 | 0.210 |

## Caveats

1. **Synthetic queries grounded on the indexed corpus measure system internals**, not generalisation to unseen user intent. The ground truth for Generator A (title) and C (scope-NP) is the source IO itself - by construction, the IO contains the query's seed text, so high recall is expected.
2. **Generator B (subject)** ground-truth is capped at 50 IOs per subject; for tags with thousands of uses, recall@100 caps at 1.0 even when the system retrieves more than 50 of them.
3. **Generator D (typo)** ground-truth is `LIKE %canonical%` against title + scope, capped at 50 - measures recall against a literal-match baseline, not against an oracle.
4. **No image strategy** - image-on-text-path is gated on `192.168.0.78` returning. The `full_hybrid` row therefore represents 4-strategy fusion, not 5-way.
5. **CPU-only inference** - embedding latency includes Ollama on `192.168.0.112` (CPU). Vector strategy timings will improve substantially when GPU returns.
6. **Run #2** will swap Generator C.1 (rule-based scope-NP) for C.2 (LLM-paraphrase via qwen3:8b), regenerated against the same query slots so ablation rows align across runs.

## Reproducing this run

```bash
# 1. Generate the simulated qrels (deterministic via seed)
php artisan ahg:discovery-simulate --persist --run-id=run-1 --seed=42 \
    --qrels-out=tests/discovery/qrels-simulated.csv

# 2. Capture environment fingerprint
php bin/discovery-benchmark.php --qrels=tests/discovery/qrels-simulated.csv \
    --output=storage/discovery-eval/run-1/environment.json

# 3. Run all 5 ablation configs
for cfg in baseline kw_entity kw_hierarchy kw_vector full_hybrid; do
  php bin/discovery-eval.php --qrels=tests/discovery/qrels-simulated.csv \
      --config=$cfg --output=storage/discovery-eval/run-1/$cfg.json --quiet
done

# 4. Build this report
php bin/discovery-ablation-report.php --dir=storage/discovery-eval/run-1 \
    --env=storage/discovery-eval/run-1/environment.json \
    --output=docs/papers/discovery-ablation-2026-04.md
```

Determinism check (`bin/discovery-eval-verify.php --runs=2`) on the `full_hybrid` config produced byte-identical metric tuples and identical per-query top-10 ordering - see `docs/papers/discovery-implementation-notes.md` for the variance-source inventory.
